blocks.text: watched Nadia rejoin the group. She sat exactly where Marcus had asked her to, just later than he'd asked.
let the fire comment go. She'd changed the subject and that was the thing that mattered — not what she said it about.
let the evening be what it was. It had been genuinely good and not everything needed to be more than that.
let the evening settle without naming it. Some things worked better that way.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: I have to sleep. I can feel it hitting.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: That's what you said when the last fire was still going.
The group filtered inside. Mara went ahead — she'd been up since five. Eventually it was just Marcus and Julian at what was left of the fire.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Ten years. You'd think it would feel less new each time. But every time we do this — just get everyone in the same place — it still gets to me a little.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: You put a lot of work into keeping people together.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: It's not work. It's just — you get the time and then it's gone again. I'd rather not waste it wondering what everyone's up to. I'd rather just know.
He meant it. There was no performance in it at all.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: There's actually something I've been meaning to get to Dean before the weekend's over. Small thing. I keep meaning to and then I forget I've got it. Probably nothing at this point.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I'd have to find it. I should just — before Sunday, I should find it.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Don't let the weekend get away from you before you do.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Anyway. I'm glad you two came over tonight. The group needed a couple of outsiders. Keeps it honest.
I'd have to find it. He said it like the thing itself was small. What he sounded like was someone who didn't know what he was carrying.

blocks.prompt: Marcus has something for Dean. He's been forgetting it. Does it mean anything?

options.label: Filed. Not significant — people have small intentions they forget all the time.
"Probably nothing" is what people say when something is something.
"Don't let the weekend get away from you." He meant it more than he let on.
